project.name: grow-app
project.description: SvelteKit/Svelte 5 site-mode HMI for a local hydroponic grow broker. The SvelteKit server keeps an MQTT session to a local Mosquitto broker, caches retained/current state, streams browser updates over SSE, and publishes commands only through discovered topics, with optional InfluxDB time-series history and its own local SQLite authentication.

nodes.name: dev-snapshot.ts
nodes.summary: Development/demo mode that loads a captured live Snapshot from a URL or file and simulates command handling against it, letting the UI run without a live MQTT broker.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves the site MQTT configuration (broker URL, credentials, topic and discovery prefixes, client id) from environment variables with sensible defaults.

nodes.name: discovery.ts
nodes.summary: Parses ESPHome/Home-Assistant-style MQTT discovery topics and payloads into normalized EntityConfig objects, flags dangerous entities, and builds validated non-retained command publishes.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of abbreviated-key fallback maps to normalize ESPHome's short discovery field names into full EntityConfig fields.

nodes.name: service.ts
nodes.summary: The long-lived server-side MQTT session that connects to Mosquitto, caches retained/current state, tracks entities/devices/firmware/UI metadata, streams SnapshotEvents to subscribers, and publishes commands only through discovered command topics.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Central TypeScript type definitions for the MQTT layer: entity/device configs, availability, device-UI metadata, firmware, snapshot, and command request/publish shapes shared across server and client.

nodes.name: ui-metadata.ts
nodes.summary: Parses retained device-UI config topics and payloads (grow-ui.v1 schema) into DeviceUiConfig groups and entities that drive dashboard and device-settings layout.

nodes.name: dev-snapshot.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for the dev-snapshot simulation, covering snapshot loading edge cases (bad HTTP, thrown fetch, malformed shape, disabled config) and command validation results without publishing.

nodes.name: discovery.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests covering MQTT discovery parsing, device-UI metadata parsing, duplicate ESPHome unique-id scoping, and non-retained command-publish building.

nodes.name: devSnapshotConfig
nodes.summary: Builds the dev-snapshot configuration (enabled flag, snapshot URL/file source, command mode) from environment variables.

nodes.name: loadDevSnapshot
nodes.summary: Loads a Snapshot from the configured URL or file and validates its shape, returning null on any failure or when disabled.

nodes.name: devSnapshotCommandResult
nodes.summary: Simulates a command against the loaded snapshot, returning HTTP-style results (404 unknown entity, 503 unavailable) or a built command publish without touching a live broker.

nodes.name: getSiteMqttConfig
nodes.summary: Resolves the SiteMqttConfig (broker URL, credentials, topic/discovery prefixes, client id) from environment variables with defaults derived from the site name.

nodes.name: normalizeDiscoveryId
nodes.summary: Normalizes an arbitrary discovery identifier into a lowercase underscore-delimited slug.

nodes.name: parseDevice
nodes.summary: Parses the device block of a discovery payload into a DiscoveryDevice, normalizing identifiers and resolving name/manufacturer/model fields.

nodes.name: parseDiscoveryTopic
nodes.summary: Splits a discovery topic under the configured prefix into its component, nodeId, and objectId parts, rejecting topics outside the prefix.

nodes.name: parseDiscoveryPayload
nodes.summary: Parses a discovery topic and payload into a normalized EntityConfig, resolving abbreviated ESPHome fields and marking dangerous and writable entities.

nodes.name: isDangerousEntity
nodes.summary: Flags entities whose name or object id suggests a destructive action (reset, reboot, factory) so the UI can require confirmation.

nodes.name: buildCommandPublish
nodes.summary: Validates a command request against an entity's metadata and builds a non-retained CommandPublish, throwing on missing command topic or invalid value.

nodes.name: commandPayload
nodes.summary: Coerces a requested value into the MQTT payload string appropriate for the entity component (switch on/off, number, select option, button press).

nodes.name: SiteMqttService
nodes.summary: Long-lived MQTT service that maintains the broker connection, caches entities/devices/state/firmware/UI metadata, emits SnapshotEvents to subscribers, and performs command, firmware, and camera-frame operations.

nodes.name: getSiteMqttService
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and starts the process-wide SiteMqttService singleton using the resolved site MQTT config.

nodes.name: parseGroup
nodes.summary: Parses a single device-UI group entry (title, order, variant, surface, section) applying defaults for order and open state.

nodes.name: parseEntity
nodes.summary: Parses a single device-UI entity placement entry (component, objectId, group, role, order, label) into a DeviceUiEntity.

nodes.name: parseUiConfigTopic
nodes.summary: Extracts the device nodeId from a retained UI-config topic sitting under the configured topic prefix.

nodes.name: parseUiConfigPayload
nodes.summary: Parses a retained grow-ui.v1 UI-config payload into a DeviceUiConfig of groups and entities, treating empty retained payloads as deletions.

nodes.name: camera.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright e2e spec verifying the thermal camera image tile renders in the dashboard THERMAL panel, that its quick controls POST entity commands, and that the camera is absent from the AtomS3U device-settings page. Mocks /api/snapshot with the dashboard fixture and serves a thermal.jpg for the image route.

nodes.name: dashboard.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Playwright e2e suite for the scan-focused HMI dashboard, exercising live metrics, URL-driven device-settings navigation, alert-threshold slider dragging with clamp/scaling behavior, the thermal single-band alarm card and buzzer test, and the full firmware update lifecycle. Stubs the SSE EventSource and snapshot/command HTTP routes.
nodes.languageNotes: Injects a mock EventSource into the page via addInitScript to deterministically drive the SSE state stream, and simulates pointer-drag gestures for range sliders.

nodes.name: design-screenshots.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright spec that captures full-page dashboard and device-settings screenshots from the live snapshot fixture, attaching them to the test report for Penpot design import.

nodes.name: dashboard-snapshot.ts
nodes.summary: Hand-crafted Snapshot fixture exporting `dashboardSnapshot`, a deterministic broker/entity state used across the camera and dashboard e2e specs to render the HMI without a live MQTT session.
nodes.languageNotes: Typed against the `Snapshot` interface from the MQTT server module so the fixture stays in sync with the real state shape.

nodes.name: live-snapshot.ts
nodes.summary: Large sanitized snapshot (~130 entities from the real daniel-home Atlas hydro kit + AtomS3U rig) exporting `liveSnapshot`, used by the screenshot specs so generated design references mirror production. LAN IPs, MACs, and wifi identifiers are redacted; re-captured via scripts/capture-fixture.sh.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated captured data fixture (5300+ lines of a single typed object literal); treated as opaque test data rather than structural code.

nodes.name: mission-control-screenshots.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright screenshot spec capturing the dashboard plus the four device-settings sections (calibration, alerts, controls, updates) from the live snapshot, producing mission-control design reference images with synthesized history point series.

nodes.name: installMockEventSource
nodes.summary: Injects a page init script that replaces the browser EventSource with a controllable mock, letting tests deterministically feed SSE snapshot updates to the dashboard.

nodes.name: dragSliderBy
nodes.summary: Simulates a pointer drag of a threshold slider handle by a pixel delta, with an optional callback fired before releasing to assert mid-drag state.

nodes.name: dragSliderTo
nodes.summary: Drags a slider handle from its current center to a target x-coordinate, used to move alert thresholds to specific scaled positions.

nodes.name: captureThresholdCommands
nodes.summary: Intercepts entity command POST routes and records the published threshold values so tests can assert clamped and scaled outputs.

nodes.name: access.ts
nodes.summary: Server-side guard that authenticates firmware-update HTTP requests by constant-time comparison of a query-string token against the configured FIRMWARE_UPDATE_TOKEN secret.

nodes.name: metadata.ts
nodes.summary: Parses firmware device and channel MQTT topic payloads and builds channel config objects, normalizing retained broker state into typed records.

nodes.name: packages.ts
nodes.summary: Core firmware package subsystem that fetches, selects, downloads, and checksum-validates device firmware packages from either Codeberg generic packages or a GHCR OCI registry, and maps them into ESPHome web-flasher update manifests.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a discriminated FirmwarePackageProvider union to switch between the Codeberg generic-packages API and the OCI distribution API; the OCI path implements the registry bearer-token challenge/response handshake and layer-digest resolution.

nodes.name: update-state.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export that surfaces the shared firmware update-state parser and its type to server code from the isomorphic $lib/firmware module.

nodes.name: firmware.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit suite exercising the firmware package pipeline: channel version selection, Codeberg and OCI listing/download, checksum validation, ESPHome manifest mapping, and update-token access control.

nodes.name: requireFirmwareUpdateToken
nodes.summary: Validates the token query parameter against the FIRMWARE_UPDATE_TOKEN secret using constant-time comparison, returning a 503/401 JSON error Response or the accepted token.

nodes.name: firmwareDeviceTopic
nodes.summary: Extracts the node ID from a firmware device MQTT topic, validating the configured prefix and suffix.

nodes.name: firmwareChannelTopic
nodes.summary: Extracts the node ID from a firmware channel MQTT topic against the configured topic prefix.

nodes.name: parseFirmwareDevicePayload
nodes.summary: Parses a retained firmware device MQTT payload into a normalized FirmwareDeviceConfig, tolerating malformed JSON and missing fields.

nodes.name: parseFirmwareChannelPayload
nodes.summary: Parses a firmware channel MQTT payload into a channel/updatedAt record, validating the channel against the allowed enum.

nodes.name: buildFirmwareChannelConfig
nodes.summary: Builds a firmware channel config object for a node from a channel selection, stamping an updated timestamp.

nodes.name: getFirmwarePackageSource
nodes.summary: Reads environment and secret-file variables to construct the active firmware package source config for either the Codeberg or GHCR OCI provider.

nodes.name: packageDownloadUrl
nodes.summary: Builds the Codeberg generic-package download URL for a given owner, package, version, and filename.

nodes.name: listCodebergPackages
nodes.summary: Paginates the Codeberg packages API to collect all firmware package versions for an owner, following Link rel=next headers until exhausted.

nodes.name: listOciFirmwarePackages
nodes.summary: Lists firmware version tags from a GHCR OCI registry tag-list endpoint, mapping them into package listings.

nodes.name: selectPackageVersion
nodes.summary: Selects the newest package version for the requested channel, sorting stable semver descending or edge builds by embedded timestamp.

nodes.name: downloadPackageManifest
nodes.summary: Downloads and parses the firmware package manifest for a device/version, dispatching to the OCI path when the source provider is GHCR.

nodes.name: resolveFirmwarePackage
nodes.summary: Orchestrates end-to-end firmware resolution: lists packages, selects the channel version, downloads the manifest, and asserts it matches the requested device.

nodes.name: toEspHomeManifest
nodes.summary: Transforms an internal firmware package manifest into the ESPHome web-flasher update manifest shape with a tokenized OTA download path.

nodes.name: downloadAndValidateBinary
nodes.summary: Downloads the OTA binary via a Codeberg URL or an OCI layer and validates the bytes against the manifest md5/sha256 checksums.

nodes.name: validateBinaryChecksums
nodes.summary: Verifies downloaded firmware bytes against the manifest sha256 and md5 digests, throwing on any mismatch.

nodes.name: parsePackageManifest
nodes.summary: Validates and narrows an unknown JSON payload into a typed FirmwarePackageManifest, enforcing schema and required-field invariants.

nodes.name: assertPackageManifestMatchesDevice
nodes.summary: Asserts a resolved manifest's device, channel, and package-owner fields match the request, guarding against serving mismatched firmware.

nodes.name: ociFetch
nodes.summary: Performs an OCI registry request, transparently handling 401 WWW-Authenticate bearer challenges by fetching a token and retrying.

nodes.name: fetchRegistryToken
nodes.summary: Exchanges an OCI bearer challenge for a registry access token via the realm/service/scope token endpoint, adding basic auth when configured.

nodes.name: downloadOciPackageManifest
nodes.summary: Downloads the firmware package manifest layer from an OCI image and parses its decoded JSON body.

nodes.name: downloadOciLayer
nodes.summary: Fetches an OCI image manifest, resolves the layer digest for a target filename, and downloads the blob bytes.

nodes.name: ociLayerDigest
nodes.summary: Resolves the digest of the OCI layer whose title annotation matches the requested filename.

nodes.name: parseCodebergPackage
nodes.summary: Narrows a raw Codeberg package API item into a typed CodebergPackage listing, returning null when malformed.

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves InfluxDB connection config from environment variables (with a Docker-secret `_FILE` fallback) and hands out a lazily-cached InfluxDB client, degrading to null when the site has no time-series backend deployed.
nodes.languageNotes: Secrets support a `<NAME>_FILE` indirection so Docker/Compose file-mounted secrets work alongside plain env vars.

nodes.name: query.ts
nodes.summary: Builds and executes the Flux query that pulls windowed historical readings for a set of (node, entity) series from InfluxDB, returning downsampled time-series buckets in the original request order.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a TypeScript type-guard (`isHistoryRange`) and Flux `aggregateWindow` to cap each series near TARGET_POINTS for uPlot rendering.

nodes.name: trend-domains.ts
nodes.summary: Maps a trend domain (water/climate/thermal/substrate) to the concrete (node, entity) Influx series to chart, reusing the dashboard's device resolvers so trend charts and readouts always plot the same device.

nodes.name: history.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for the history subsystem, exercising the range/domain type guards and asserting that `resolveDomainSeries` maps water/climate/thermal domains to the expected entities against a captured live snapshot fixture.

nodes.name: secretEnv
nodes.summary: Reads a secret from an env var, falling back to the contents of the `<NAME>_FILE` path so file-mounted Docker/Compose secrets are supported transparently.

nodes.name: getInfluxConfig
nodes.summary: Assembles the InfluxDB connection config from environment variables, returning null when URL or token is absent so callers can degrade gracefully instead of throwing.

nodes.name: getInfluxDB
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches a single InfluxDB client from the resolved config, or returns null when the backend is unconfigured.

nodes.name: isInfluxConfigured
nodes.summary: Boolean helper reporting whether a valid InfluxDB config is present in the environment.

nodes.name: isHistoryRange
nodes.summary: Type guard validating that a string is one of the supported history range presets (1h/3h/6h/12h/24h).

nodes.name: queryHistory
nodes.summary: Builds a parameterized Flux query with aggregateWindow downsampling, streams matching rows from InfluxDB, and buckets values back into per-series point arrays preserving the caller's request order.

nodes.name: metricSpecs
nodes.summary: Projects a device's firmware-declared numeric dashboard metrics into series specs (key/label/unit/node/entity), dropping any entry missing node or entity tags.

nodes.name: resolveDomainSeries
nodes.summary: Resolves a trend domain to the concrete Influx series to plot, delegating to shared device resolvers for water/climate and scoping thermal to a single device's MLX90640 array-temp entities.

nodes.name: live-snapshot-context.ts
nodes.summary: Svelte context helpers that provide and read the single shared live-snapshot store, ensuring one SSE connection is shared across the whole page tree.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a module-private Symbol as the context key and a ReturnType<typeof createLiveSnapshot> alias so the provider and consumers stay type-linked.

nodes.name: live-snapshot.svelte.ts
nodes.summary: Client-side live-snapshot state store built on Svelte 5 runes; defensively normalizes incoming snapshots, subscribes to the /api/events SSE stream, and exposes reactive state plus command-publishing helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Runes ($state, $state.snapshot) are used outside a component in a .svelte.ts module; the factory returns getter-based accessors to carry reactivity across the module boundary.

nodes.name: live-snapshot.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for normalizeSnapshot, verifying that array and map fields stay defined against a fallback snapshot and default to empty collections when no fallback is provided.

nodes.name: setLiveSnapshotContext
nodes.summary: Stores the shared live-snapshot store under a Symbol key in Svelte context so descendant pages and components share the single SSE-backed store.

nodes.name: getLiveSnapshot
nodes.summary: Reads the shared live-snapshot store from Svelte context for use in any descendant page or component.

nodes.name: brokerOr
nodes.summary: Coerces an unknown value into a valid BrokerSnapshot, falling back per-field to cloned defaults for any missing or mistyped connection-status property.

nodes.name: firmwareOr
nodes.summary: Coerces an unknown value into a FirmwareSnapshot with defined devices and channels maps, cloning fallbacks when the input is malformed.

nodes.name: normalizeSnapshot
nodes.summary: Defensively normalizes an unknown value into a complete Snapshot, guaranteeing every field is present by merging typed fallbacks and cloning array and map fields.

nodes.name: createLiveSnapshot
nodes.summary: Factory that builds the reactive live-snapshot store: holds rune-based $state, connects to the /api/events SSE stream to apply snapshot/entity/state/availability/broker/firmware events, and exposes getters plus stateFor, formatState, and sendCommand helpers.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the grow-app SvelteKit HMI: sets up Node 24 + pnpm, installs frozen dependencies, builds the app plus a standalone history-recorder sidecar, and assembles a pruned production runtime image running as a non-root user.
nodes.languageNotes: Four stages (base -> deps -> build -> runtime) minimize final image size: dev dependencies are pruned and only the build output plus production node_modules are copied into a clean node:24-bookworm-slim runtime.

nodes.name: base
nodes.summary: Base stage on node:24-bookworm-slim that enables corepack and activates pnpm 11.5.3, establishing the shared toolchain and /app workdir for later stages.

nodes.name: deps
nodes.summary: Dependency stage that copies the package manifests and lockfile, then runs a frozen-lockfile pnpm install for reproducible dependency resolution that caches independently of source changes.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Build stage that copies the full source, runs svelte-kit sync and the SvelteKit production build, bundles the standalone history-recorder sidecar (esbuild is a dev dep), then prunes to production-only dependencies.

nodes.name: runtime
nodes.summary: Final production runtime stage that copies the build output and pruned node_modules into a clean node:24-slim image, provisions a node-owned /data volume for the SQLite auth DB, exposes port 3000, and runs the server as the non-root node user.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context exclusion list that keeps node_modules, build artifacts, test output, local data, secrets (.env), and VCS metadata out of the image build context to speed builds and avoid leaking local state.

nodes.name: build.yaml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes the grow-app container image to GHCR by delegating to the reusable dephekt/ci-templates docker-build workflow. Triggers on pushes to main, version tags, and pull requests when Dockerfile, source, or build config change.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a reusable workflow (`uses:` at the job level) so the build/publish logic is centralized in an external ci-templates repository rather than duplicated per project.

nodes.name: ci.yaml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs quality gates on pull requests and pushes to main: a check job for typecheck and unit tests, and an e2e job running Playwright browser tests. Uses pnpm store caching and concurrency cancellation for in-progress runs.
nodes.languageNotes: Both jobs share an identical pnpm bootstrap sequence (corepack enable, resolve store path, actions/cache) — a copy-paste pattern common in Actions since composite step reuse is not used here.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Single build job that invokes the reusable docker-build workflow with contents:read and packages:write permissions to build and publish ghcr.io/dephekt/grow-app from the node upstream image.

nodes.name: check
nodes.summary: Typecheck and unit-test job on ubuntu-latest that sets up Node 24 and pnpm, restores the pnpm store cache, installs with a frozen lockfile, then runs `pnpm check` and `pnpm test`.

nodes.name: e2e
nodes.summary: Playwright end-to-end job that installs Chromium with dependencies, runs the Playwright suite with GitHub and HTML reporters, and uploads the HTML report as an artifact with a 7-day retention.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Annotated environment-variable template for grow-app, documenting site/MQTT connection, local-account and OIDC auth, TLS-proxy forwarding headers, InfluxDB history, and firmware OTA settings. Every secret has a `*_FILE` variant for Docker secret mounts.
nodes.languageNotes: Each sensitive value offers a `*_FILE` variant so the container reads the secret from a mounted file instead of an inline env, aligning with Docker/Compose secret conventions.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Contributor/agent guide enforcing a Svelte 5 runes-only guardrail (with a Svelte 4-vs-5 idiom table), defining site-mode scope boundaries, and pointing to the committed understand-anything knowledge graph for orientation.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project overview and operations guide covering local development, live-snapshot mode, MQTT/InfluxDB configuration, app-owned authentication, the production GHCR container, and the media-stack LAN deployment/update workflow.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root manifest for the private ESM SvelteKit project, pinning pnpm@11.5.3 and Node >=24, and defining dev/build/check/test/e2e scripts plus SvelteKit, MQTT, InfluxDB client, uPlot, and Vite dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `"type": "module"` (ESM) and a dedicated `build:recorder` esbuild step that bundles the history recorder into a standalone Node ESM entrypoint with a `$lib` alias.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: Single-package pnpm workspace declaration rooting the workspace at the repo root and allow-listing esbuild's postinstall build script.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration extending SvelteKit's generated tsconfig with strict type checking, JS checking, bundler module resolution, and source maps for the site HMI codebase.
nodes.languageNotes: Extends `./.svelte-kit/tsconfig.json` (SvelteKit-generated) and enables `checkJs` with `moduleResolution: bundler`, so plain JS files are type-checked under the same strict rules.

nodes.name: .npmrc
nodes.summary: npm/pnpm project config setting engine-strict so installs fail on an unsupported Node/pnpm engine rather than warning.

nodes.name: .thumbnail
nodes.summary: Opaque encoded thumbnail asset for the mission-control design folder; a design artifact with no runtime role in the app.

nodes.name: MissionControl.dc.html
nodes.summary: Standalone high-fidelity HTML prototype of the redesigned Dashboard and Device Settings screens; a look-and-behavior design reference rendered by support.js, explicitly not shipped in the app.
nodes.languageNotes: Authored with custom <x-dc> component tags and x-for/x-if/x-import directives that only the sibling support.js runtime interprets.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Design brief for the 'Mission Control' HMI redesign: the dark instrument-panel visual language, the curate-else-fallback principle, per-screen layout, intended fonts, and guidance for recreating it in the SvelteKit codebase.

nodes.name: support.js
nodes.summary: Standalone client runtime that parses the .dc.html prototype's custom component/directive markup and renders it with React; a design-tool artifact needed only to open the mockup and irrelevant to the Svelte implementation.
nodes.languageNotes: Bundled IIFE implementing a mini template compiler (walk/compileTemplate over x-for/x-if/x-import) plus a React component-factory and Babel-based x-import loader.

nodes.name: auth.setup.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright setup project that logs in as the seeded local admin through the real login form and persists the resulting session cookie as shared storage state for the app test projects.

nodes.name: auth.spec.ts
nodes.summary: End-to-end specs exercising the auth wall from a clean storage state: unauthenticated redirect to /login, wrong-password rejection, login/logout, local-password change, admin user creation, and the public /health endpoint.

nodes.name: playwright.config.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright configuration that builds and previews the app with an ephemeral seeded auth DB, then defines a login 'setup' project plus desktop/tab5/phone browser projects that reuse the saved authenticated storage state.

nodes.name: capture-fixture.sh
nodes.summary: Operator script that pulls a live /api/snapshot and one thermal frame from the deployed site via docker exec, then runs the sanitizer to regenerate the committed Playwright screenshot fixture.

nodes.name: sanitize-fixture.mjs
nodes.summary: Sanitizes a captured /api/snapshot into a committable typed Snapshot fixture: redacts wifi SSID/BSSID/MAC/IP identity sensors, rewrites RFC1918 LAN IPs to TEST-NET addresses, and strips verbose discovery payloads.
nodes.languageNotes: Regex-based redaction with a fixed MAC-to-placeholder map, guarding against collapsing distinct device MACs to a single placeholder.

nodes.name: app.css
nodes.summary: Global 'Mission Control' dark-theme stylesheet: self-hosted IBM Plex Sans/Mono font imports and CSS custom properties defining surfaces, text, status accents, radii, and typography tokens.

nodes.name: app.d.ts
nodes.summary: SvelteKit ambient type declarations extending App.Locals with the per-request authenticated user (or null) set by the auth guard in hooks.server.ts.

nodes.name: app.html
nodes.summary: SvelteKit HTML shell template with favicon links and the head/body placeholders into which the app is server-rendered.

nodes.name: hooks.server.ts
nodes.summary: SvelteKit server entry that warms the MQTT singleton and bootstraps the local admin at startup, and installs the auth request guard enforcing session resolution, CSRF, protected-path access, and rolling session renewal.

nodes.name: handle
nodes.summary: Per-request handle hook: resolves and clears the session cookie, sets locals.user, enforces same-origin JSON CSRF on mutating API/auth requests, redirects or 401s unauthenticated access to protected paths, and renews rolling sessions.

nodes.name: AccountMenu.svelte
nodes.summary: Account dropdown menu with a set/change-local-password modal dialog; tracks hasLocalPassword locally so the dialog mode reacts to load-data refreshes and cancels stale auto-close timers on reopen.

nodes.name: AlertsPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Curated alerts UI that derives threshold rules by pairing threshold number entities with matching alert binary sensors by metric prefix, rendering draggable low/high bands plus single-band alarm extras (buzzer mute, alarm test).

nodes.name: CalibrationPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Guided probe-calibration panel (pH/EC/RTD/ORP) that detects probe type and calibration steps from device entities and walks the operator through buffer-solution steps with live-reading stabilization.

nodes.name: CameraImageTile.svelte
nodes.summary: Thermal/camera image tile that polls the entity image endpoint on an interval with cache-busting, surfaces freshness/failure state, and renders optional collapsible quick controls.

nodes.name: CommandBar.svelte
nodes.summary: Top command bar showing site identity, broker connection status, an update indicator, a live clock, dashboard/settings navigation, and the embedded account menu.

nodes.name: ReadoutPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Compact dashboard readout table rendering labelled value rows with status dots, an optional device id, and a dashed 'PLANNED' placeholder badge for not-yet-available domains.

nodes.name: ThermalPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Dashboard thermal panel that locates the camera-bearing device, derives its curated camera presentation, and renders the thermal camera tile with its quick controls.

nodes.name: TrendsPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Dashboard trends panel with domain and time-range selectors that race-guardedly fetches history series from /api/history and short-circuits placeholder ('planned') domains without hitting the API.

nodes.name: device-presentation.ts
nodes.summary: Derives the curated Dashboard and Device Settings presentations from a snapshot, mapping firmware UI config (entity roles, groups, surfaces) into metric/quick-control/camera and sectioned settings panels with a graceful raw-entity fallback.

nodes.name: dashboardPresentation
nodes.summary: Builds the dashboard presentation (metrics, quick controls, cameras) from a device's entities and firmware UI config, attaching camera-group controls to their camera; falls back to a plain metric/camera list when no config exists.

nodes.name: presentedNumericMetrics
nodes.summary: Returns a device's numeric dashboard metrics (role:metric filtered to numeric sensors), optionally stripping a label prefix; the single source shared by the WATER/CLIMATE readouts and their trend series.

nodes.name: deviceSettingsPresentation
nodes.summary: Builds the Device Settings panels: groups non-dashboard entities into inferred settings sections, sweeps unconsumed entities into Diagnostics/Other, and buckets everything into the fixed top-level panels; falls back to controls/maintenance/diagnostics when uncurated.

nodes.name: fallbackSettingsPresentation
nodes.summary: Fallback Device Settings layout for devices without a firmware UI config: splits entities into Controls, Maintenance (dangerous), and Diagnostics sections.

nodes.name: inferDeviceSettingsSection
nodes.summary: Maps a device UI group to one of the fixed settings sections, honoring an explicit deviceSettingsSection then inferring from id/title keywords (threshold, calibration, maintenance, control, diagnostic).

nodes.name: entity-match.ts
nodes.summary: Shared entity recognisers and device resolvers so the dashboard and history layers agree on which device is the hydro controller vs the air rig and on what counts as the pH/CO₂/ambient-temperature reading.
nodes.languageNotes: Type-only import of the MQTT types keeps the module client-safe (no server-only runtime code).

nodes.name: isAmbientTemperature
nodes.summary: Recognises a genuine ambient (room/air) temperature sensor: requires a real temperature signal and excludes water probes, board/internal temps, and derived aggregates (min/max/mean/avg).

nodes.name: isCo2
nodes.summary: Recognises a CO₂ sensor by carbon_dioxide device class, a co2 objectId token, or a co2/carbon-dioxide name.

nodes.name: deviceOwning
nodes.summary: Resolves the device that owns the first entity matching a predicate, keyed by the entity's nodeId.

nodes.name: resolveWaterDevice
nodes.summary: Resolves the WATER (hydro controller) device, preferring the pH probe's device and falling back to the water-temperature probe's device.

nodes.name: resolveClimateDevice
nodes.summary: Resolves the CLIMATE (air rig) device, preferring CO₂, then humidity, then a bare ambient-temperature sensor.

nodes.name: resolveThermalDevice
nodes.summary: Resolves the THERMAL device carrying the MLX90640 thermal array.

nodes.name: EntityRow.svelte
nodes.summary: Generic entity row rendering an entity's label/metadata and the appropriate control (switch toggle, number input, etc.), emitting commands and formatting state via the shared state-format helper.

nodes.name: FirmwareUpdatesPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Svelte 5 panel that surfaces per-device firmware status: reconciles installed vs latest version, looks up available packages, renders the changelog, and issues channel/check/apply commands.

nodes.name: Sparkline.svelte
nodes.summary: Lightweight inline-SVG sparkline component that plots a numeric series as an auto-scaled line and optional filled area.

nodes.name: ThermalCameraControls.svelte
nodes.summary: Svelte 5 control panel for a thermal camera exposing palette, overlay, and region-of-interest controls with row/column bounds, delegating commands to a parent handler.

nodes.name: TrendsChart.svelte
nodes.summary: Svelte 5 wrapper around uPlot that renders multi-series time-series trend charts, resolving line colors from theme CSS variables.
nodes.languageNotes: Caches theme CSS vars once per page lifetime to avoid getComputedStyle on every structural rebuild.

nodes.name: firmware.ts
nodes.summary: Client-side firmware metadata parsers: extracts a project version from an ESPHome sw_version string and normalizes the Home Assistant update-entity payload into a typed update state.

nodes.name: audit.ts
nodes.summary: Server-side auth audit logger that appends security events (user creation, password changes, logins) to the auth_audit table, swallowing write errors so auditing never breaks the caller.

nodes.name: authz.ts
nodes.summary: Authorization helper that throws a JSON 401/403 response unless the request locals carry an authenticated admin user.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Central auth configuration: cookie name/TTL constants, env-driven settings (db path, site slug, bootstrap admin, SSO toggle), HTTPS detection, and the session cookie option builder.

nodes.name: db.ts
nodes.summary: Opens and migrates the SQLite auth database (users, sessions, audit), applies WAL/foreign-key pragmas, and schedules periodic purging of expired sessions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses better-sqlite3 with a PRAGMA user_version-based forward-only migration guard.

nodes.name: guard.ts
nodes.summary: Request-guard predicates for the auth hook: classifies paths as public/device-token/protected, checks safe HTTP methods, and enforces CSRF safety via content-type and Sec-Fetch-Site.

nodes.name: passwords.ts
nodes.summary: Scrypt-based password hashing and verification, encoding salt and cost parameters in the stored string and comparing digests in constant time.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Node crypto scryptSync plus timingSafeEqual for timing-safe verification.

nodes.name: sessions.ts
nodes.summary: Session lifecycle over SQLite: mints random tokens, persists only their SHA-256 hash, looks up and renews sessions with sliding expiry, and revokes sessions per token or per user.

nodes.name: users.ts
nodes.summary: User store for the auth subsystem: row-to-DTO mappers, lookups by username/id/OIDC, local user creation, password and disabled-state mutations, and bootstrap-admin provisioning.

nodes.name: parseProjectVersion
nodes.summary: Extracts the semantic project version from an ESPHome sw_version string via regex, returning null when no version is present.

nodes.name: parseFirmwareUpdateState
nodes.summary: Parses the Home Assistant update-entity JSON payload into a typed firmware update state (installed/latest version, release summary), falling back to an empty state on malformed input.

nodes.name: recordAudit
nodes.summary: Inserts a security audit row (timestamp, event, actor, ip, detail) into auth_audit, logging and swallowing any write failure so auditing never breaks the caller.

nodes.name: requireAdmin
nodes.summary: Throws a JSON 401 when no user is authenticated and 403 when the user is not an admin; returns the admin user otherwise.

nodes.name: getBootstrapAdmin
nodes.summary: Reads the optional bootstrap admin username and password from env (supporting _FILE secret indirection) used to seed the first admin account.

nodes.name: isSecureRequest
nodes.summary: Determines whether a request arrived over HTTPS by inspecting the x-forwarded-proto header, which drives the Secure cookie flag.

nodes.name: sessionCookieOptions
nodes.summary: Builds the session cookie attribute set (httpOnly, sameSite, path, maxAge, secure) from the resolved security context.

nodes.name: migrate
nodes.summary: Applies forward-only schema migrations to the auth database guarded by PRAGMA user_version, creating the users, sessions, and audit tables.

nodes.name: openAuthDb
nodes.summary: Opens the SQLite auth database (creating its parent directory as needed), enables WAL and foreign keys, and runs migrations.

nodes.name: purgeExpiredSessions
nodes.summary: Deletes all sessions whose expires_at has passed and returns the number of rows removed.

nodes.name: getAuthDb
nodes.summary: Lazily opens a singleton auth DB handle, runs an initial expired-session purge, and schedules a daily unref'd purge interval.

nodes.name: classifyPath
nodes.summary: Classifies a request path as public, device-token, or protected using an exact-match set and device-token URL patterns.

nodes.name: isCsrfSafe
nodes.summary: Returns whether a state-changing request is CSRF-safe based on its content-type and Sec-Fetch-Site header.

nodes.name: hashPassword
nodes.summary: Hashes a password with scrypt using a fresh random salt and encodes salt, cost parameters, and digest into a single storable string.

nodes.name: parse
nodes.summary: Parses a stored scrypt hash string back into its salt, cost parameters, and digest, validating the structure.

nodes.name: verifyPassword
nodes.summary: Verifies a candidate password against a stored scrypt hash using timing-safe comparison, tolerating malformed stored values.

nodes.name: createSession
nodes.summary: Generates a random session token, stores its SHA-256 hash with metadata (user, login method, TTL, user agent, IP), and returns the raw token.

nodes.name: lookupSession
nodes.summary: Resolves a raw token to its session and joined user row, returning null when the session is missing or expired.

nodes.name: renewIfNeeded
nodes.summary: Extends a session's sliding expiry and last-seen timestamp when it falls within the renewal window.

nodes.name: toUserSummary
nodes.summary: Maps a raw users row into the public UserSummary DTO (id, username, admin/disabled flags, timestamps).

nodes.name: toAuthenticatedUser
nodes.summary: Maps a raw users row into the AuthenticatedUser shape carried in request locals.

nodes.name: createLocalUser
nodes.summary: Creates a local (password) user by hashing the password, inserting the row, recording an audit event, and returning the new user.

nodes.name: setPassword
nodes.summary: Sets or replaces a user's password hash, stamps password_updated_at, and records an audit event.

nodes.name: clearPassword
nodes.summary: Clears a user's password hash (disabling local login) and records an audit event.

nodes.name: ensureBootstrapAdmin
nodes.summary: Ensures at least one enabled admin with a password exists, creating or promoting the configured bootstrap admin when none is present.

nodes.name: env.ts
nodes.summary: Server env helpers: env() reads a named variable, and secretEnv() adds Docker/K8s _FILE secret indirection, reading the referenced file when a NAME_FILE variable is set.

nodes.name: http.ts
nodes.summary: Maps firmware operation errors to appropriate HTTP JSON responses based on the error message, with a configurable fallback status.

nodes.name: recorder.ts
nodes.summary: Standalone history recorder sidecar that subscribes to the site MQTT snapshot, coerces recordable entity states to numeric readings, and writes them as InfluxDB points with backfill and buffering.
nodes.languageNotes: Long-running Node daemon with graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown and an unref'd flush interval.

nodes.name: state-format.ts
nodes.summary: Formats an entity's raw state value for display, applying the entity-declared numeric precision and passing through non-numeric strings.

nodes.name: threshold-match.ts
nodes.summary: Heuristics that classify Home Assistant entities by objectId/name into thresholds, alerts, buzzer switches, and alarm-test buttons, and derive a metric prefix and high/low side.

nodes.name: trends.ts
nodes.summary: Trend domain constants and types: the selectable trend domains, the default domain, and a type guard for validating a domain value.

nodes.name: +layout.svelte
nodes.summary: Authenticated app-group layout that creates one shared live snapshot (a single SSE connection) provided via context to every page and renders the command bar.

nodes.name: +layout.ts
nodes.summary: App-group load function that gates access: fetches /api/me, redirects unauthenticated users to login with an encoded return path, then loads the initial snapshot.

nodes.name: +page.svelte
nodes.summary: Dashboard home page that resolves water and climate devices from the live snapshot and renders readout, trends, and thermal panels from firmware-declared metrics.

nodes.name: +page.svelte
nodes.summary: Device settings page that selects a device from URL/state and renders entity, calibration, alerts, and firmware-update panels while detecting available firmware updates.

nodes.name: +page.ts
nodes.summary: Client-only load for the device-settings page that reads the selected device and section from URL search params.

nodes.name: +page.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin user management page that lists users and provides create, password-reset, admin-toggle, and disable/delete actions against the /api/users endpoint.

nodes.name: env
nodes.summary: Returns the value of a named environment variable or undefined.

nodes.name: secretEnv
nodes.summary: Resolves a secret from either NAME or a NAME_FILE variable, reading and trimming the referenced file for Docker/K8s secret mounts.

nodes.name: firmwareError
nodes.summary: Translates a firmware operation error into an HTTP JSON response, mapping known messages to statuses like 404/409 and defaulting to a fallback status.

nodes.name: numericValue
nodes.summary: Coerces an entity state into a numeric reading, mapping on/off-style booleans to 1/0 and rejecting non-finite values.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Recorder entry point that connects to InfluxDB and the site MQTT snapshot, backfills and streams entity readings as points, and installs buffering plus graceful shutdown.

nodes.name: formattedNumericValue
nodes.summary: Formats a numeric value to a fixed precision, truncating to an integer when precision is zero and returning null for non-numeric input.

nodes.name: formatEntityState
nodes.summary: Formats an entity's state for display, applying the entity's declared numeric precision or passing the raw value through.

nodes.name: metricPrefix
nodes.summary: Derives the base metric name for a threshold entity by stripping high/low/min/max and threshold/alert/limit suffixes from its objectId.

nodes.name: entitySide
nodes.summary: Determines whether a threshold entity represents the high or low side from its objectId/name tokens.

nodes.name: isThresholdEntity
nodes.summary: Returns whether an entity is a numeric threshold control based on its objectId tokens.

nodes.name: isAlertEntity
nodes.summary: Returns whether an entity represents an alert/alarm indicator based on objectId and name heuristics.

nodes.name: load
nodes.summary: Auth-gating loader that calls /api/me, redirects unauthenticated visitors to the login page with an encoded return path, then fetches the initial live snapshot.

nodes.name: /api/entities/[entityId]/command
nodes.summary: POST endpoint that publishes an entity command over MQTT to the site broker; short-circuits to a synthetic result in dev-snapshot mode and maps an offline broker to a 503.

nodes.name: /api/entities/[entityId]/image
nodes.summary: GET endpoint returning an entity's latest camera frame as raw image bytes fetched from the MQTT service, or 404 when no frame is cached.

nodes.name: /api/events
nodes.summary: Server-Sent Events stream that pushes an initial site snapshot then live updates via the MQTT service subscription with a periodic heartbeat, falling back to a snapshot-only stream in dev mode.

nodes.name: /api/firmware/devices/[nodeId]/apply
nodes.summary: POST endpoint that validates a device against its resolved firmware package and current update state, then triggers the OTA apply on the MQTT firmware entity.

nodes.name: /api/firmware/devices/[nodeId]/binary/[filename]
nodes.summary: Device-token-authenticated GET endpoint that streams the validated OTA firmware binary after checking the package manifest matches the requesting device.

nodes.name: /api/firmware/devices/[nodeId]/channel
nodes.summary: GET/PUT endpoint reading and updating a device's selected firmware release channel via the MQTT firmware entity.

nodes.name: /api/firmware/devices/[nodeId]/check
nodes.summary: POST endpoint that resolves the latest firmware package for a device's channel and triggers an update check, reporting whether a newer version is available.

nodes.name: /api/firmware/devices/[nodeId]/manifest
nodes.summary: Device-token GET endpoint returning the ESPHome-format update manifest for a device's resolved firmware package.

nodes.name: /api/firmware/devices/[nodeId]/package
nodes.summary: GET endpoint that resolves and returns firmware package metadata for a device, optionally for a specified channel.

nodes.name: /api/history
nodes.summary: GET endpoint returning trend history series for a requested range and domain, querying InfluxDB when configured and shaping entity specs from the current site snapshot.

nodes.name: /api/me
nodes.summary: Public pre-session GET endpoint returning the current authenticated user from request locals and whether SSO is enabled; consumed by the login page loader.

nodes.name: /api/snapshot
nodes.summary: GET endpoint returning the current site snapshot from the MQTT service, or a dev snapshot when running in dev mode.

nodes.name: /api/users/[id]
nodes.summary: Admin-only PATCH endpoint to update a user — toggling disabled state (revoking sessions) or clearing a password — with audit logging.

nodes.name: /api/users
nodes.summary: Admin-only user management endpoint: GET lists users, POST creates a local user with validation and duplicate-username handling.

nodes.name: /auth/login
nodes.summary: POST endpoint that authenticates a username/password against the local auth DB, creates a session, sets the session cookie (Secure decided from x-forwarded-proto), and audits failures and successes.

nodes.name: /auth/logout
nodes.summary: POST endpoint that deletes the current session and clears the session cookie, writing a logout audit record.

nodes.name: /auth/password
nodes.summary: POST endpoint letting the signed-in user change their own password after verifying the current one and enforcing the minimum length.

nodes.name: /health
nodes.summary: Unauthenticated GET liveness/readiness probe reporting broker connectivity derived from the current site snapshot.

nodes.name: svelte.config.js
nodes.summary: SvelteKit configuration selecting the Node adapter, Vite preprocessing, and Svelte 5 runes mode for the HMI build.
nodes.languageNotes: Enables Svelte 5 runes via compilerOptions.runes and targets adapter-node to emit a standalone Node server.

nodes.name: +page.svelte
nodes.summary: Public login page — a Svelte 5 runes form that POSTs credentials to /auth/login, surfaces errors, and offers an SSO link when enabled; hard-navigates on success so the new session cookie applies.

nodes.name: +layout.svelte
nodes.summary: Root layout that imports global app.css and renders the route children; the single app shell for both route groups.

nodes.name: +layout.ts
nodes.summary: Root layout load config disabling SSR app-wide (client-rendered HMI); inherited by both the (app) and (auth) route groups.

nodes.name: +page.ts
nodes.summary: Load function for the users admin page that fetches /api/users, redirecting non-admins to / and rendering an error page on other failures.

nodes.name: +page.ts
nodes.summary: Login page loader that resolves a safe post-login redirect target (open-redirect hardened) and checks /api/me to bounce already-authenticated visitors away from the login page.
nodes.languageNotes: sanitizeNext strips tab/newline and rejects protocol-relative and backslash variants to prevent open-redirect via ?next=.

nodes.name: auth-guard.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for the auth guard — path classification (public/protected/device-token), safe-method and CSRF checks, and Secure-cookie detection from x-forwarded-proto.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Parses the command body, returns a dev result in snapshot mode, otherwise publishes the command via the MQTT service and maps a disconnected broker to a 503.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Fetches the entity's latest camera frame from the MQTT service and returns it as image bytes with a content-type header, or 404 when absent.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Builds a ReadableStream that emits an initial snapshot and subscribed live events as SSE, with a heartbeat interval and unsubscribe cleanup on cancel.

nodes.name: snapshotOnlyStream
nodes.summary: Dev-mode SSE builder that emits a single snapshot event plus periodic heartbeats without opening a live MQTT subscription.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Loads the firmware device, resolves its package, validates the parsed update state, and invokes applyFirmwareUpdate, translating firmware errors into HTTP responses.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Verifies the device update token, resolves the package source, downloads and validates the binary against its manifest, and streams the bytes to the device.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Returns the device's currently selected firmware channel read from the MQTT firmware snapshot.

nodes.name: PUT
nodes.summary: Validates the requested firmware channel and persists it on the device via the MQTT firmware entity.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Resolves the latest package for the device's channel and triggers a firmware check, returning update-availability details.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Token-guarded handler returning the ESPHome update manifest for the device's resolved firmware package.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Resolves firmware package metadata for the device, honoring an optional channel query parameter.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Validates range/domain query params, resolves entity series from the site snapshot, and returns Influx-backed history points shaped per spec.

nodes.name: PATCH
nodes.summary: Admin handler that disables/enables or clears the password of a target user, revoking its sessions and recording an audit entry.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Admin handler validating and creating a local user, rejecting duplicate usernames and passwords below the minimum length.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Verifies credentials against the auth DB, creates a session, sets the session cookie with the correct Secure flag, and audits the login outcome.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Deletes the active session, clears the session cookie, and records a logout audit entry.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Verifies the current password and minimum length before setting the signed-in user's new password.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Reads the site snapshot and reports broker connectivity as a health status payload.

nodes.name: load
nodes.summary: Fetches /api/users, redirecting non-admins and surfacing errors, returning the user list for the admin page.

nodes.name: load
nodes.summary: Computes a sanitized redirect target and checks /api/me, redirecting already-authenticated visitors away from the login page.

nodes.name: auth-passwords.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the scrypt-based password hashing helpers, verifying correct-password round-trips, per-call salt uniqueness, the encoded stored-string format, and rejection of wrong, tampered, null, and malformed hashes.

nodes.name: auth-sessions.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the session lifecycle against an in-memory SQLite auth database, asserting token-hash-only storage, session lookup, renewal, expired-session purging, and per-user deletion.

nodes.name: auth-users.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for local user management against temp-file SQLite databases, covering user creation, initial-admin bootstrap, username lookup, and password verification.

nodes.name: device-presentation.test.ts
nodes.summary: Extensive unit tests for the device-presentation layer, asserting how MQTT entities are grouped and shaped into dashboard cards and device-settings sections, including camera frame-source resolution and numeric-metric presentation.

nodes.name: state-format.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for entity state formatting, verifying discovery-precision rounding of numeric values, pass-through of nonnumeric states, and unit suffixing behavior.

nodes.name: threshold-match.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the threshold/alert entity classification helpers, covering alert, threshold, buzzer-switch, and alarm-test-button recognition plus metric prefixing and high/low side detection for thermal and scd4x entity shapes.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite/Vitest configuration that registers the SvelteKit plugin and scopes the Vitest run to the tests/unit/**/*.test.ts suite.

nodes.name: makeAtomEntity
nodes.summary: Test factory that builds a fully-populated EntityConfig fixture with sensible AtomS3U defaults, overridable per test case.

nodes.name: makeSnapshot
nodes.summary: Test factory that assembles a Snapshot fixture from the given UI configs with a current timestamp, overridable per test case.

nodes.name: mk
nodes.summary: Test factory that constructs EntityConfig fixtures for threshold/alert classification cases, filling default MQTT discovery fields around the caller-supplied component and objectId.

layers.name: Routing & API Endpoints
layers.description: SvelteKit server request surface: the JSON/SSE API endpoints (entities, events, firmware, history, me, snapshot, users), session form-action endpoints, the health probe, and the global hooks.server.ts request guard that resolves sessions and enforces protected paths.

layers.name: UI Components & Pages
layers.description: Browser-rendered Svelte 5 HMI: reusable panel components (alerts, calibration, camera, command bar, trends, firmware updates, thermal controls), dashboard sub-panels, the authenticated and login route pages/layouts, and the app.html/app.css shell.

layers.name: Client State & Presentation Logic
layers.description: Browser-side non-component modules that shape device data for the UI: the live-snapshot reactive store/context, entity and threshold matching, trend and device-presentation helpers, client firmware and state formatting, plus SvelteKit page/layout load functions and the app.d.ts ambient types.

layers.name: Server / MQTT & Device Integration
layers.description: Server-only core that maintains the Mosquitto session, performs discovery, caches retained/current device state and publishes commands only through discovered topics (SiteMqttService), plus shared server runtime helpers (env config, dev snapshot).

layers.name: Authentication & Accounts
layers.description: Self-contained local login subsystem: SQLite-backed accounts and audit log, scrypt password hashing, session issuance/renewal, the request guard, and authorization/config for the app-owned auth flow.

layers.name: Time-Series History
layers.description: Optional InfluxDB time-series persistence: the Influx client, trend queries and domain mapping, and the recorder sidecar that streams MQTT state into InfluxDB.

layers.name: Firmware Delivery
layers.description: Server-side firmware-package pipeline: fetching and caching OCI packages from Codeberg/GHCR, manifest/metadata parsing, access control, HTTP transport, and per-device update-state tracking.

layers.name: Build & CI/CD Infrastructure
layers.description: Containerization and continuous integration: the multi-stage Dockerfile and its stages, .dockerignore, and the GitHub Actions build/CI workflows with their check, e2e, and image-build jobs.

layers.name: Automated Tests & QA Tooling
layers.description: Vitest unit specs for server and client logic, Playwright end-to-end specs with their auth setup and snapshot fixtures, the Playwright config, and the fixture capture/sanitize scripts that produce real-data snapshots.

layers.name: Configuration, Docs & Design Reference
layers.description: Non-runtime project material: build/tooling config (package.json, tsconfig, Vite, Svelte, pnpm workspace, .npmrc, .env.example), the README/AGENTS docs, and the standalone Mission Control design mockup used only as a visual reference.

tour.title: Project Overview & Svelte 5 Rules
tour.description: Start with the README to learn what grow-app is: a SvelteKit site-mode HMI where the server owns a long-lived MQTT session to a local Mosquitto broker, caches device state, streams it to browsers over SSE, and enforces its own login on every request. AGENTS.md then sets the ground rules every later step depends on: this is a Svelte 5 runes-only codebase with a strict Svelte-4-vs-5 idiom table and site-mode scope boundaries. Read these two documents first so the architecture that follows has context.
tour.languageLesson: Svelte 5 replaces the Svelte 4 reactive model wholesale: `$state()` for reactive variables, `$derived()` for computed values, `$props()` for component inputs, and `$effect()` for side effects. This project bans the old `export let`, `$:`, and store-`$` auto-subscription idioms entirely — expect runes everywhere.

tour.title: Server Entry & Auth Guard
tour.description: src/hooks.server.ts is the single entry point every request passes through. At startup it warms the MQTT singleton and bootstraps the local admin account; on each request it installs the auth guard that resolves the session cookie, enforces CSRF, blocks protected paths, and renews sessions with a sliding expiry. guard.ts holds the predicates it delegates to: classifying a path as public (only /health, /favicon.ico, the login page, and firmware device-token endpoints), device-token, or protected. This is where the request lifecycle begins.
tour.languageLesson: A SvelteKit `handle` hook in hooks.server.ts wraps every request. It receives `{ event, resolve }`, can mutate `event.locals` (here, the authenticated user) before calling `resolve(event)`, and can short-circuit with a `Response` to reject a request before it ever reaches a route — the natural home for a global auth guard.

tour.title: Authentication & Accounts
tour.description: Building on the guard from Step 2, this is the self-contained login subsystem the app owns instead of delegating to a proxy. db.ts opens and migrates a SQLite database (users, sessions, audit) with WAL pragmas; users.ts is the account store and bootstrap-admin provisioner; passwords.ts hashes with scrypt; sessions.ts mints random tokens but persists only their SHA-256 hash and renews them with sliding expiry. config.ts (the highest fan-in file in this layer) centralizes cookie TTLs, the HttpOnly/Secure cookie builder, and env-driven settings. Together they explain how a session cookie becomes the `locals.user` the guard trusts.
tour.languageLesson: Two security idioms recur here: passwords are verified with `crypto.timingSafeEqual` (constant-time compare defeats timing attacks), and only the SHA-256 hash of each session token is stored — a database leak never exposes a usable cookie value.

tour.title: MQTT Integration & Discovery
tour.description: This is the server's beating heart and the most depended-upon cluster in the graph. SiteMqttService (service.ts) holds the long-lived Mosquitto connection, caches retained and current device state, tracks entities/devices/firmware, streams SnapshotEvents to subscribers, and publishes commands ONLY through topics it discovered — the browser never touches MQTT directly. discovery.ts parses ESPHome/Home-Assistant discovery topics into normalized EntityConfig objects and flags dangerous entities; ui-metadata.ts parses retained grow-ui.v1 config that drives dashboard layout; config.ts resolves broker settings from the environment; types.ts is the shared vocabulary consumed on both server and client.
tour.languageLesson: SiteMqttService is a module-level singleton warmed once in hooks.server.ts, not a per-request object. That single shared session is what lets one MQTT subscription fan out to many browser SSE streams — a common Node server pattern for a stateful, long-lived resource behind a stateless request layer.

tour.title: API Endpoints & the SSE Stream
tour.description: The route endpoints are where an authenticated request meets the MQTT service from Step 4. /api/snapshot returns the current cached site snapshot in one GET; /api/events is the real-time channel — a Server-Sent Events stream that pushes an initial snapshot then live updates via an MQTT subscription, with a heartbeat to keep the connection alive. /api/entities/[entityId]/command is the only write path: it publishes a command over MQTT through a discovered command topic and maps an offline broker to a 503. Each falls back to a dev snapshot when running without a live broker.
tour.languageLesson: SSE (Server-Sent Events) is a one-way server→browser stream over a plain HTTP response with `Content-Type: text/event-stream`. Here it fits perfectly: state flows one direction (broker→server→browser) via /api/events, while commands flow back through a separate ordinary POST, keeping the browser fully decoupled from MQTT.

tour.title: Client Live-Snapshot Store
tour.description: On the browser side, live-snapshot.svelte.ts is a Svelte 5 runes store that subscribes to the /api/events SSE stream, defensively normalizes each incoming snapshot into reactive state, and exposes command-publishing helpers. live-snapshot-context.ts shares that single store through Svelte context so the whole page tree reuses one SSE connection. The presentation helpers turn raw entities into UI meaning: device-presentation.ts derives the curated dashboard and settings layout from firmware UI config, entity-match.ts agrees on which device is the hydro controller vs. the air rig, and threshold-match.ts classifies entities into thresholds, alerts, and alarm controls.
tour.languageLesson: A `.svelte.ts` file is how Svelte 5 lets runes live outside a component: the `$state`/`$derived` inside this module are compiled into reactive state, so the store is reactive anywhere it is imported. Paired with `setContext`/`getContext`, it guarantees exactly one shared store — and thus one SSE connection — for the entire page tree.

tour.title: Dashboard UI & Pages
tour.description: Now the rendered HMI. The authenticated (app) layout creates the single shared live snapshot from Step 6, provides it via context, and renders the command bar; its sibling load function gates access by redirecting unauthenticated users to login. The dashboard +page.svelte resolves the water and climate devices from the snapshot and composes three panels: ReadoutPanel (labelled value rows with status dots and 'PLANNED' placeholders), TrendsPanel (domain/time-range selectors that fetch history), and ThermalPanel (the camera tile). This is the firmware-declared, presentation-driven layout coming together on screen.
tour.languageLesson: SvelteKit route groups in parentheses — `(app)` and `(auth)` — organize routes without adding a URL segment. The `(app)` group carries the shared layout and its auth-gating `load`, so every page inside it inherits the login requirement and the single live-snapshot context for free.

tour.title: Time-Series History & Recorder
tour.description: History is optional and degrades gracefully. influx/client.ts resolves InfluxDB config from the environment (with a Docker-secret `_FILE` fallback) and returns null when no time-series backend is deployed; query.ts builds the windowed Flux query; trend-domains.ts maps a domain like water/climate to concrete (node, entity) series, reusing the same device resolvers as the dashboard so charts and readouts always plot the same device. The /api/history endpoint serves the TrendsPanel from Step 7. Separately, recorder.ts is a standalone sidecar that subscribes to the MQTT snapshot and writes numeric readings into InfluxDB — the write half of the history story.
tour.languageLesson: The `_FILE` env-var convention (read the value from a file path when `X_FILE` is set instead of `X`) is the Docker-secrets idiom: secrets are mounted as files rather than baked into the environment. Returning `null` when unconfigured lets the whole history feature stay optional without conditionals scattered across callers.

tour.title: Firmware Delivery
tour.description: grow-app also serves OTA firmware to its devices, fetched server-side so the browser and devices never reach the registry directly. packages.ts is the core: it fetches, selects, downloads, and checksum-validates firmware from Codeberg generic packages or a GHCR OCI registry, then maps them into ESPHome web-flasher manifests. metadata.ts normalizes retained firmware/channel MQTT topics into typed records; access.ts guards the update endpoints by constant-time-comparing a query-string token (these are the device-token paths the Step 2 guard lets through unauthenticated). The /api/firmware/.../apply endpoint triggers an update, and FirmwareUpdatesPanel.svelte surfaces per-device status, changelogs, and channel/check/apply controls.

tour.title: Real-Data Fixtures & E2E Tests
tour.description: This project prefers real captured data over hand-built fixtures. e2e/fixtures/live-snapshot.ts is a large sanitized snapshot (~130 entities from the real hydro kit, with LAN IPs/MACs redacted) that drives the Playwright screenshot specs so generated design references mirror production. capture-fixture.sh is the operator tool that re-captures it from the deployed site and runs the sanitizer. auth.spec.ts is the end-to-end login test (and the spec that hooks.server.ts is `tested_by`), and playwright.config.ts wires the browser test run. Together they show how the app is verified against production-shaped data.

tour.title: Build, Container & Deployment
tour.description: Finally, how grow-app ships. The multi-stage Dockerfile sets up Node 24 + pnpm, installs frozen dependencies, builds the SvelteKit app plus the standalone history-recorder sidecar from Step 8, and assembles a pruned production image running as a non-root user. build.yaml publishes that image to GHCR via a reusable workflow, while ci.yaml runs the quality gates (typecheck + unit tests, then Playwright e2e). package.json pins the toolchain and scripts, and .env.example documents every setting — MQTT, auth, InfluxDB, firmware — each with a `_FILE` secret variant for the LAN media-stack Compose deployment.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use several `FROM` stages: dependency install and compilation happen in heavy builder stages, then only the pruned runtime artifacts are copied into a slim final image. Ordering layers from rarely-changing (base, deps) to frequently-changing (app source) maximizes Docker's build-cache reuse.
